titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5969

respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=758 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 21:10:50.584252+00 | translation moa=759 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 21:10:50.597554+00

div.p:
Omnibus Christi fidelibus ad quos presens scriptum peruenerit . Hugo leuedyman de lagehope et eua vxor sua
soror laurenci fili Willelmi fili Gocelini de Berewic salutem . Nouerit vniuersitas uestra nos ad instantiam dicti
laurenci Concessisse . et hac presenti carta nostra confirmasse . quietam clamasse . et de nobis et heredibus nostris In perpe
tuum omnino abiurasse Donationem et Concessionem Totius terre sine aliquo Retinemento In territorio de
Prendergest quam laurencius filius Willelmi filii Gocelini de Berewic habuit de dono Roberti fratris sui . quam
idem laurencius dedit . Concessit . et carta sua confirmauit deo . sancte Ebbe . Ricardo priori et conuentui de col
dingham . et eorum successoribus In liberam . puram . et perpetuam elemosinam . Afidauimus eciam et Tactis sacrosanctis
Iurauimus pro nobis et heredibus nostris quod nullo tempore seculi contra donacionem et Concessionem predic
tam venire presumemus . In Cuius Rei Testimonium  presenti scripto sigilla nostra apposuimus . Testibus  Domino Willelmo de Mordingtona . Domino dauid de Haddingtona . Domino Radfulfo nobili . Domino Willelmo de scremerstona .
Ada de Prendergest’ . Ada filio Iohannis de superyory Eytona . Thoma de Nesbyt . Bertramo de Ristona . Patricio
scotto . Gilberto de lumesden’ . Iohanne filio Ingrami . Gregorio de Betyoc . et Aliis

To all Christ's faithful to whom the present written document shall come, Hugh Levedyman of Langehope, and Eve his wife, sister of Laurence son of William son of Gocelin of Berwick, send greeting. Know us, all of you, to have granted at the urging of the said Laurence, and have made firm by this our present charter, quitclaimed, and for ourselves and our heirs for ever, entirely abjured the donation and grant of the whole land, without any withholding, in the territory of Prenderguest which Laurence, son of William son of Gocelin of Berwick, had from the gift of Robert his brother, which the same Laurence gave, granted, and by his own charter made firm to God, St Ebbe, Richard the prior, and the convent of Coldingham and their successors, in free, pure, and perpetual alms. We have also pledged and sworn, touching holy relics, for ourselves and our heirs, that we shall in no era of the world seek to move against the aforesaid donation and grant. As witness of this matter, we have applied our seals to the present document. As witnesses, the lord William of Mordington, the lord David of Haddington, the lord Ralph Noble, the lord William of Scremerston, Adam of Prenderguest, Adam son of John of Over Ayton, Thomas of Nisbet, Bertram of Reston, Patrick Scott, Gilbert of Lumsdaine, John son of Ingram, Gregory of Bethock, and others.
